question.text: A merchant banker is advising an acquirer who holds 32% voting rights. The acquirer purchased 2% in Q1 and 2% in Q2 of the same financial year. If the acquirer wishes to buy 2% more in Q3, what must be done under SAST Regulations?

answer.text: Make a public announcement of an open offer since cumulative gross acquisition would exceed 5% in the financial year
No action needed since total holding remains below 40%
File a disclosure with the stock exchange only
Seek prior SEBI approval before purchasing

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The acquirer holds above 25% and has already acquired 4% (2% + 2%) in the financial year. Buying 2% more would bring gross acquisitions to 6%, exceeding the 5% creeping acquisition limit under Regulation 3. This triggers a mandatory open offer requirement. **The Trap:** "No action needed since total holding remains below 40%" ignores the 5% annual creeping acquisition limit. The threshold is not about absolute holding but about the annual acquisition quantum. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX frequently presents scenarios requiring candidates to track cumulative acquisitions through a financial year — a practical skill for merchant bankers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Maintain a running tracker of all acquisitions by your client and PACs through the financial year. The 5% limit can be breached unexpectedly when multiple small acquisitions accumulate.
